title: Unifying a $30M Jewellery Operation
content:
## The Problem

Trezza Jewels began as a focused operation with a small team handling orders end to end. As the business expanded, fulfilling a single order naturally started to involve multiple internal teams, external vendors, and retail partners.

Teams were often working in parallel, across multiple revisions, with frequent back and forth. The existing setup was built on email, spreadsheets, and shared folders. While this worked at an earlier stage, it was not designed to support complex, non-linear workflows at scale.

This engagement focused on creating a single operating system that could support real operational complexity without adding overhead or friction.

---

## The Starting Point at Trezza Jewels

Trezza Jewels relied on a familiar set of tools:

- Email for coordination and approvals  
- Spreadsheets to track order status  
- Shared folders to store files and revisions  

This setup was functional, but increasingly fragile as order volumes increased and customization became the norm.

---

## Limitations of Email and Spreadsheet-Based Order Tracking

### No single source of truth

Order details, updates, approvals, and files were spread across multiple tools. Teams did not have a reliable way to see the current state of an order without cross-checking emails, sheets, and folders.

### Access and continuity risks

Managing access to shared spreadsheets and folders required constant manual effort. Changes were difficult to trace, and employee onboarding or exits created ongoing access and continuity risks.

### Coordination depended on email discipline

Progress relied heavily on correct email threads and people being copied at the right time. Important updates were often buried in inboxes rather than living alongside the order itself.

### File version confusion

Multiple versions of the same file existed at the same time. It was not always clear which version was under review, approved, or final, leading to unnecessary rework and follow-ups.

### Inability to support parallel team workflows

Multiple teams often needed to work on the same order at the same time. Spreadsheet tracking forced each order into a single status, even when work was happening concurrently across teams.

---

## Objectives for a Scalable Order Management System

The goal was to design a system that would:

- Act as the single source of truth for orders, files, and communication  
- Support parallel work across multiple teams  
- Maintain clarity without forcing rigid, linear workflows  
- Give leadership real-time visibility into operations  

All while reducing reliance on manual coordination.

---

## Centralized Order and Version Management

<video
  src="/videos/Orders,-Items,-Versions.mp4"
  autoplay
  loop
  muted
  playsinline
  preload="none"
  style="width:100%; border-radius:12px;">
</video>

All orders now live in one system, structured around a hierarchy of orders -> items -> versions. Every update, file, comment, and approval is directly linked to the relevant order or version.

This creates a clear, traceable history and removes ambiguity for all teams involved.

---

## Role-Based Access and Team-Specific Views

<video
  src="/videos/Roles-Users-Team-Page.mp4"
  autoplay
  loop
  muted
  playsinline
  preload="none"
  style="width:100%; border-radius:12px;">
</video>

Each team member has a dedicated login and sees only the information relevant to their role. This reduces noise, improves focus, and makes access management straightforward as teams evolve.

---

## Supporting Asynchronous, Multi-Team Workflows

<video
  src="/videos/Async-Workflow.mp4"
  autoplay
  loop
  muted
  playsinline
  preload="none"
  style="width:100%; border-radius:12px;">
</video>




Orders can hold multiple active statuses at the same time. Each status represents work required from a specific team, allowing the same order to appear as active work for multiple teams simultaneously.

This supports true parallel execution without manual handoffs or follow-ups.

---

## Embedded Communication Tied to the Work

<video
  src="/videos/Comments.mp4"
  autoplay
  loop
  muted
  playsinline
  preload="none"
  style="width:100%; border-radius:12px;">
</video>

Comments, updates, and notifications happen directly within the context of the order. Communication lives with the work instead of being scattered across inboxes.

This significantly reduces reliance on email for day-to-day coordination.

---

## Vendor and Retailer Collaboration

<video
  src="/videos/Vendor-Portal.mp4"
  autoplay
  loop
  muted
  playsinline
  preload="none"
  style="width:100%; border-radius:12px;">
</video>

External vendors and retail partners can submit requests, track live order status, review and approve work at a version level, and follow shipping updates.

All collaboration remains structured, contextual, and traceable.

---

## Customer-Facing Order Visibility

<video
  src="/videos/Customer-Portal.mp4"
  autoplay
  loop
  muted
  playsinline
  preload="none"
  style="width:100%; border-radius:12px;">
</video>

Retailers can share a clean, visual view of each order with their customers, including items, versions, and real-time progress.

This replaces forwarded emails and screenshots with a clear and professional experience.

Since the system operates on a single source of truth, there is no gap between internal teams, retailers and customers anymore.
---

## Real-Time Operational Visibility for Leadership

<video
  src="/videos/Manager-Dashboard.mp4"
  autoplay
  loop
  muted
  playsinline
  preload="none"
  style="width:100%; border-radius:12px;">
</video>


Leadership has a live view of order status across teams, current workloads, and emerging bottlenecks without relying on manual reporting.

---

## Business Impact for Trezza Jewels

- A single source of truth across teams and partners  
- Reduced operational risk as order complexity increases  
- Faster execution through parallel workflows  
- Clear ownership and accountability  
- Improved collaboration with retailers  

---

## Key Takeaway

This engagement aligned the system with how the business actually operates.

By replacing fragmented tools with a unified order management system and formally supporting multi-team, asynchronous workflows, Trezza Jewels moved from informal coordination to structured execution.

The result is a calmer, more predictable operation built to scale.

title: Tracking Inventory Across The Globe
content:
**Client:** A precious metal manufacturer
**Industry:** Jewelry Manufacturing

# Connecting Two Countries, One Gram at a Time

## The Problem

The client buys raw gold, silver, and platinum from suppliers in the US, ships it to India for casting, then ships finished pieces back to the US as sellable goods.

The entire operation ran on:

- Email threads for shipment notifications
- Spreadsheets for tracking inventory
- WhatsApp messages for urgent metal requests

This created three recurring issues.

**1. No real time visibility.** Nobody could say, with confidence, how much metal was actually on hand at the casting facility. Someone had to manually count and cross check files.

**2. No audit trail.** A shipment, the castings it produced, and the consumption that used it up lived in three different places, if they were recorded at all.

**3. Slow coordination.** When the India team ran low on a specific metal purity, the request often got buried in a chat thread for a day or two before anyone acted on it.

Small discrepancies compounded over weeks into reconciliation headaches that took days to untangle.

## The Approach

We mapped the actual flow of metal through the business, not the flow on paper, but what was really happening day to day.

That became a six step workflow built into the platform:

1. **Raise requirement.** India's manufacturing team flags what metal, what purity, and how much is needed
2. **Create shipment.** US sourcing team reviews the requirement, builds the shipment, uploads documentation
3. **Create casting.** Sourcing team defines SKUs and bag sizes before release
4. **Report consumption.** Manufacturing team logs metal used, against the specific casting it came from
5. **Reconcile and release.** Finance verifies the casting against its invoice before it becomes usable
6. **Live balance.** System tracks on hand metal in real time, throughout

Three things we built underneath that workflow:

- **Full traceability.** Every consumption record maps back to its casting, which maps back to its shipment. Either team can trace the chain in either direction.
- **Bulk operations.** Shipments, castings, and consumption can all be imported and exported in bulk, useful for migrating off spreadsheets without re-keying everything by hand.
- **Safe bulk consumption.** If a bulk upload includes consumption against metal that isn't available, the system flags it, skips it, and never lets the balance go negative. It returns a log of exactly which consumption rows couldn't be processed, so nothing is silently lost or wrongly deducted.

Role based access was built in from day one through a central admin panel, so sourcing, manufacturing, and finance each see only what's relevant to them.

## The Result

- **One system of record** replacing spreadsheets, email, and WhatsApp for the entire cross border operation
- **Real time inventory** instead of manual counts
- **100% traceability** from shipment to casting to consumption
- **A financial control gate** so nothing becomes usable inventory before finance verifies it against an invoice
- **Safe bulk uploads** that never overdraw inventory, with a clear log of anything that couldn't be consumed

What started as a fix for one client's cross border coordination problem has become the foundation for a platform now being positioned for other manufacturers running the same kind of operation: buying raw precious metal in one country, casting it in another, and needing one system both teams can trust.
